channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: BeyondTrust, LastPass Impacted by Klue-Salesforce Incident
item.description: Over a dozen Klue customers have confirmed that hackers stole data from their Salesforce instances. The post BeyondTrust, LastPass Impacted by Klue-Salesforce Incident appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: This flying solar-powered platform could deliver better internet from the air
item.description: As soon as August, a giant silver bullet will cut its way through the dry air of the southwestern US and cross the Pacific to reach the coast of Japan.  Once there, the roughly 200-foot-long craft, built by the New Mexico–based company Sceye, will park some 18 kilometers above the ocean’s...

item.title: All challenges big and small
item.description: When I was 18, I skipped my high school graduation and headed to Kuwait. It was 1991, the first Gulf War had just ended, and the country was in complete chaos. There was little to no electricity, aside from generator power. Rubble and unexploded ordnance were everywhere. Massive oil fires lit up...

item.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Tuesday announced the seizure of a cloud computing account put to use by subsidiaries of Cambodia-based corporate conglomerate HuiOne Group, as the Treasury unveiled fresh sanctions against nine individuals and 26 entities linked to Prince Group. "These...

item.title: Tencent QQ Mail Launches Agently Mail, Enabling Autonomous Email and Business Coordination for AI Agents
item.description: Tencent has launched Agently Mail, a dedicated email service purpose-built for AI agents, now in beta testing. The product directly addresses two core pain poin...

item.title: Life's New Algorithm: AI Reconstructs the Full Healthcare Industry Chain at 36Kr WAVES2026
item.description: As artificial intelligence increasingly permeates the healthcare sector, the fundamental question of how it restructures the entire medical value chain took cen...

item.title: China's Large Scientific Facilities Adopt a New KPI: Industrial Impact at HEPS Synchrotron
item.description: HEPS fourth-generation synchrotron light source shifts focus from publications to industrial applications in batteries, aviation and chips

item.title: Behind Kimi K2.7 Code: An Overlooked New Paradigm in AI Coding Is Taking Shape
item.description: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code shifts AI coding from generating code to reconstructing behavior from existing products

item.title: Tsinghua Ecosystem Sets Its Sights on World Models as the Next AI Frontier
item.description: Zhipu AI, Shengshu Tech, Momenta and other Tsinghua-affiliated companies pursue world models across video, robotics and autonomous driving

item.title: Webinar Today: Modern Exposure Validation in the AI Era
item.description: The exploit timeline collapsed. Make sure your validation didn't. The post Webinar Today: Modern Exposure Validation in the AI Era appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] Claude Tag: Multiplayer, Proactive, Persistent Agents in Slack
item.description: Claude finally gets a Slackbot upgrade

item.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
item.description: Threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME). The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230 (CVSS score: 8.6), is a case of...

item.title: gpt-4o-mini high error rate
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Images (Operational) Chat Completions (Operational) Realtime (Operational) Embeddings (Operational) Moderations (Operational) Responses (Operational) Login (Operational) Batch (Operational) Audio (Operational)...

item.title: Hackers Exploiting Cisco Unified CM Vulnerability
item.description: Cisco noted that a PoC had been available for CVE-2026-20230 when it announced patches in early June. The post Hackers Exploiting Cisco Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: RIFT-Bench: Dynamic Red-teaming For Agentic AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.23927v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are rapidly evolving into autonomous decision-making systems, exposing attack vectors beyond those of traditional LLM vulnerabilities. Existing security evaluations are often tied to specific...

item.title: Neuro-Symbolic Drive: Rule-Grounded Faithful Reasoning for Driving VLAs
item.description: arXiv:2606.23938v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Driving VLA models incorporating Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning are attractive because they leverage pretrained VLM representations and expose intermediate decisions in natural language, yet current rationales often lack the step-by-step decision...

item.title: Critique of Agent Model
item.description: arXiv:2606.23991v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What is an agent? What constitutes agency? With the rise of Large Language Model (LLM) systems marketed as ``coding agents'', ``AI co-scientists'', and other ``agentic" tools that promise to drive up productivity, and at the same time, ``existential"...

item.title: Safe and Generalizable Hierarchical Multi-Agent RL via Constraint Manifold Control
item.description: arXiv:2606.24010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems are widely used in safety-critical applications that require coordinated behavior under strict safety constraints. Existing approaches face a fundamental trade-off: learning-based methods achieve strong empirical performance but...

item.title: Reinforcement Learning Towards Broadly and Persistently Beneficial Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.24014v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As AI systems are deployed across increasingly diverse and high-stakes settings, model alignment must generalize beyond the tasks and domains seen during training. This is especially important for reinforcement learning (RL), which can introduce...

item.title: Systematic Exploration of 4-Expert Heterogeneous Mixture-of-Experts via Automated Pipeline Search
item.description: arXiv:2606.23739v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present an automated large-scale search pipeline for heterogeneous 4-Expert Mixture-of-Experts (MoE4) architectures within the LEMUR neural network dataset ecosystem. Building on a hand-crafted heterogeneous MoE reference model, we replace manual...

item.title: Weight-Space Geometry of Offline Reasoning Training
item.description: arXiv:2606.23740v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline reinforcement-learning losses (RFT, RIFT, DFT, Offline GRPO, DPO) are widely used to distill reasoning from large teachers into smaller students, and are typically compared on downstream accuracy alone. We ask whether they are mechanistically...

item.title: A Survey on Federated Causal Discovery and Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.23741v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal reasoning, which encompasses the discovery of causal structures and the inference of causal effects, is fundamental to data-driven decision making. In practice, data for reliable causal analysis are often distributed across institutions and...

item.title: Low-power analogue neural networks with trainable nonlinear connections for continuous control
item.description: arXiv:2606.23742v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physical neural networks promise low-power machine learning by computing directly with analogue device physics, but most architectures force nonlinear device responses to act as scalar weights. Inspired by Kolmogorov-Arnold networks, we place...

item.title: Synergizing Physically Constrained MCMC and Chemical-Informed Gaussian Processes for Reaction Network Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2606.23757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Extracting interpretable governing equations from sparse, noisy chemical time-series data remains difficult because discrete reaction topology and continuous kinetic parameters are tightly coupled. We present PC-MCMC-CIGP, a reproducible gray-box...

item.title: EXPO-SQL: Execution-based Clause-level Policy Optimization for Text-to-SQL
item.description: arXiv:2606.23693v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-SQL enables users to query databases using natural language by generating executable SQL queries. Recent methods have increasingly adopted Large Language Models based reinforcement learning (RL) to leverage execution feedback for training....

item.title: ModTGCN: Modularity-aware Graph Neural Networks for Text Classification
item.description: arXiv:2606.23694v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph-based text classification models typically rely on local neighborhood aggregation and overlook global community structure, despite semantic document graphs exhibiting strong class-consistent clustering. Ignoring this can blur class boundaries...

item.title: Quantifying Prior Dominance in RAG Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.23695v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) grounds Large Language Models in external knowledge, yet current evaluations rely on discrete heuristics that suffer from ''epistemic blindness'' - failing to distinguish genuine contextual information extraction...

item.title: Self-Recognition Finetuning can Prevent and Reverse Emergent Misalignment
item.description: arXiv:2606.23700v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emergent misalignment (EM) has been linked to the activation of misaligned persona vectors and evil character traits, suggesting that EM operates through disruption of the model's aligned character rather than direct learning of harmful content....

item.title: Evaluating LLM Usage for Efficient and Explainable Numerical and Classified Implicit Sentiment Analysis of Product Desirability
item.description: arXiv:2606.23701v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Qualitative product feedback can reveal nuanced user experiences, but its implicit sentiment is difficult to measure. This paper presents a scalable and interpretable framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to quantify product desirability...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Model Found Vulnerabilities in Classified US Government Systems, Official Says
item.description: Come vulnerabilities were found within hours, but that does not mean the model was able to exploit them within that time, the official said. The post Anthropic’s Mythos Model Found Vulnerabilities in Classified US Government Systems, Official Says appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: We are seeing elevated errors with Next Edit Suggestions and Completions
item.description: Jun 23, 23:29 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 23, 23:27 UTC Monitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to...

item.title: Heads in the game
item.description: The Argentina v. France final of the 2022 Men’s World Cup in Qatar was shaping up to be one of the most epic games in soccer history. With just 12 minutes remaining in the extra time added to the game to break a tie, the referee had a critical decision to make—and fast. Lionel Messi,…

item.title: Super Mario is mathier than you think
item.description: Here’s a problem you probably didn’t solve in school: You’re an ambitious young plumber from Brooklyn in a world inhabited by violent human-size mushrooms called Goombas. The love of your life has been kidnapped, so you embark on a quest to rescue her, venturing through stretches of pipe-filled and...

item.title: A man of many words
item.description: Brian Sietsema has a favorite word. It’s somewhat surprising that he can choose just one. He’s the person spellers rely on to confirm pronunciations and answer questions about the roots of the words they’re given at the Scripps National Spelling Bee—arguably the world’s most prestigious competition...

item.title: Claude.ai is experiencing elevated error rates
item.description: Jun 23, 18:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 23, 18:32 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 23, 18:24 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
item.description: A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally. The campaign, active since February 2026, involves collecting credential...

item.title: INC20000047
item.description: Jun 23, 17:50 UTC Update - Current status: We're continuing to investigate the issue affecting Snowpark Container Services to determine its source. We'll provide another update within 1 hour.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified region may be intermittently unable to access or...

item.title: Dragos Unveils AI for OT Security
item.description: Named EmberAI, the new capability is built on Dragos’ massive operational technology cybersecurity dataset. The post Dragos Unveils AI for OT Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Compact Object-Level Representations with Open-Vocabulary Understanding for Indoor Visual Relocalization
item.description: Indoor visual relocalization plays a critical role in emerging spatial and embodied AI applications. However, prior research was predominantly devoted to low-level vision schemes, struggling to perceive scene semantics and compositions, which limits both interpretability and applicability. In this...

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known...

item.title: Network Performance Issues - Increased HTTP 5XX Errors in Ashburn, US
item.description: Jun 23, 16:00 UTC Resolved - Between 17:07 and 17:45 UTC, Cloudflare experienced network performance issues in the Ashburn, US region, resulting in an elevated rate of 5xx errors for a subset of traffic. During this time, impacted users may have encountered intermittent connectivity issues or...

item.title: CN-NewsTTS Bench: a target-level automatic benchmark for raw-input Chinese news TTS pronunciation
item.description: Chinese news text contains dense written forms such as scores, hyphenated model names, ranges, unit symbols, percentages, English abbreviations, and mixed Chinese-Latin-digit names. These forms are frequent in real listening workflows, and a text-to-speech (TTS) system can preserve the written...

item.title: Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
item.description: Security firm AIR built a fake AI agent skill, pushed it through a popular skill marketplace and an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts. Every skill security scanner the firm tested it against marked it safe. The payload was...

item.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order on June 22 setting hard deadlines for federal agencies to move high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography. Key establishment must move by December 31, 2030; digital signatures by December 31, 2031. EO 14409 leaves...

item.title: Elevated error rate across multiple models
item.description: Jun 23, 14:25 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 23, 14:19 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Users may experience elevated errors in ChatGPT uploading and downloading files
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components File uploads (Partial outage)

item.title: The Warrant Gap: Claim-Conditioned Re-scoring for Fact-Checking
item.description: Fact-checking systems built on LLMs achieve high verdict accuracy on standard benchmarks, yet routinely output Supports labels whose cited evidence does not license the claim. Structured decomposition is the natural way to inspect those warrants, but rigid extraction protocols strip the full-claim...

item.title: To Compare, or Not to Compare: On Methodological Practices in Evaluating Social Bias
item.description: As Large Language Models are increasingly deployed in critical applications, robustly evaluating their social biases is paramount. However, the current literature suffers from widespread methodological fragmentation, which yields contradictory conclusions. This stems largely from ignoring the...

item.title: AGORA: An Archive-Grounded Benchmark for Agentic Workplace Document Reasoning
item.description: Large language models are increasingly deployed as agents that reason over documents rather than answer from parametric knowledge. We study archive-grounded reasoning: locating sparse evidence across a large, messy collection of workplace files, reconciling inconsistent terminology, units, and time...

item.title: Build real agentic apps using CUGA: two dozen working examples on a lightweight harness
